hero_headline: Holistic Healing. Deep Support. Transformational Growth with Keunshea Fleming, CSW-I
hero_intro:
Life can feel overwhelming when you're carrying the weight of trauma, chronic illness, grief, or life transitions that seem never-ending. If you feel stuck in patterns that are hard to break or disconnected from your sense of self, you're not alone. Therapy with me is a safe, nurturing space where you can slow down, feel supported, and begin healing on a deeper level—both emotionally and within your body.

As a Clinical Social Work Intern, I work primarily with adults, especially women, from diverse cultural backgrounds, with a strong commitment to supporting individuals of African descent and underserved communities. I have extensive experience helping clients navigate substance use and addiction, PTSD and complex trauma, chronic illness (including HIV/AIDS), grief and loss, peripartum depression, anxiety, and the unique challenges of caregiving. I also bring personal understanding and compassion to families navigating neurodiversity and specialized needs.
bio_paragraphs: Keunshea Fleming holds a Master of Social Work from Simmons University and is a Clinical Social Work Intern and Certified Trauma and Recovery Practitioner. She brings a unique, integrative approach to therapy that combines clinical expertise with holistic healing modalities.

Keunshea is trained in Narrative Therapy, CBT, DBT, mindfulness-based practices, and Somatic Experiencing. She also incorporates holistic techniques such as Reiki (Level 3), Sound Healing (Level 2), Breathwork, and Parts & Memory work into her practice. She is currently advancing her training in Parts and Memory and pursuing Reiki Master Teacher certification.

With a deep commitment to culturally responsive care, Keunshea is passionate about supporting women, individuals of African descent, and underserved communities. Her approach is compassionate, empowering, and transformative—helping clients heal deeply, reclaim their narratives, and step into their fullest selves.
approach_intro: I believe healing is not just about talking—it's about transforming how you experience yourself from the inside out. My approach blends evidence-based clinical practices with holistic, body-centered techniques to support healing on both a psychological and physiological level. Together, we'll work to help you feel safe within yourself, release what no longer serves you, and build a stronger, more empowered sense of self. Here's how I support my clients:

modalities.name: Narrative Therapy
modalities.description: Reframing and releasing limiting trauma narratives

modalities.name: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
modalities.description: Building emotional regulation and distress tolerance

modalities.name: Somatic Experiencing
modalities.description: Supporting the body in processing and releasing trauma

modalities.name: Parts & Memory Work
modalities.description: Exploring and healing different aspects of the self

modalities.name: Breathwork, Reiki & Sound Healing
modalities.description: Integrating holistic tools to support nervous system regulation and deep healing

who_i_help: Adults (20–50), especially women navigating trauma, anxiety, or major life transitions
Individuals of African descent seeking culturally responsive and affirming care
Clients healing from substance use, PTSD, or complex trauma
Individuals managing chronic illness, grief, or peripartum challenges
Mothers and caregivers of children with specialized or neurodiverse needs
philosophy_headline: A Space for Deep Healing and Self-Reconnection
philosophy_paragraphs: My goal is to help you reconnect with your inner sense of safety, worth, and resilience. I support clients in breaking free from subconscious patterns, processing trauma at its root, and creating meaningful, lasting change in how they experience themselves and their lives.
If you're ready to heal not just your mind—but your whole self—I'm here to walk that journey with you.
cta_headline: Take the first step on the path toward a brighter tomorrow!
